Although four out of five software companies expect AI agents to change the software landscape noticeably, almost one third of businesses are not yet technically ready to embed the technology. A large majority of software companies expect the software landscape to change noticeably due to “AI agents” — smart programs that can perform complex tasks independently. text: One in five software companies are considering a link with such AI agent platforms. One quarter are not focusing on specific platforms, but want to make their own software “agent-ready” in a broader sense. These entrepreneurs make functions available modularly, ensure clear documentation, and set up their software so that instructions from external agents — whether or not in natural language — can easily be translated into concrete actions within the application. “As a software company, you must make sure that LLMs can talk to your back end,” summarises Dennis De Reus of Stellar. He is co-founder of Stellar, a platform that allows companies to develop customer service agents themselves. Almost one third of companies are focusing more broadly on improving data access, integration options and standardised communication with other systems (“interoperability”).
